Phase-56.NAME: Premium Public Site and Auth
Phase-56.objective: Build a premium public entry and auth experience that presents KrotPN clearly while preserving verified-email gates.
Phase-56.non-goals: Changing registration backend semantics, billing catalog ownership, deploy/install scripts, or MTProto/VPN provisioning.

step-1.action: Design public entry route with KrotPN offer, VPN value, free MTProto proxy value, tariffs, and CTA.
step-1.acceptance: frontend/src/pages/Landing.tsx renders the public / route with direct registration/login CTA, truthful VPN/MTProto copy, email-proof guard copy, and canonical three-plan tariff preview.

step-2.action: Retune login/register/reset/verify screens with premium compact Matrix style and visible logo.
step-2.acceptance: Login, register, forgot/reset password, and verify-email routes show the public logo asset and keep compact Matrix auth forms readable.

step-3.action: Preserve spam-folder guidance, duplicate-email recovery CTA, and strong-password example.
step-3.acceptance: Phase-44 and Phase-46 smoke passed after Phase-56; registration still has spam guidance, duplicate-email recovery link, password policy example, and client-side strength checks.

step-4.action: Preserve no-access-before-email-verification language and side-effect guards.
step-4.acceptance: Public copy states access activates only after email proof; registration still stores no JWT before verification and verify-email navigates to the protected dashboard only after successful token consumption.

step-5.action: Render tariff previews from existing canonical catalog shape without frontend price authority.
step-5.acceptance: Landing tariff preview mirrors M-068 1/6/9, 369/693/936 RUB, 30-day catalog and never creates payment or owns checkout amount/metadata.

step-6.action: Verify phone/tablet/desktop first viewport and reduced-motion auth readability.
step-6.acceptance: Chrome headless screenshots were captured for /, /login, /register, /forgot-password, /reset-password, and /verify-email at phone/tablet/desktop sizes plus reduced-motion 390x844; representative frames were visually reviewed.

step-7.action: Create Phase-56 smoke markers for public routes, auth states, tariff canonicality, and email-proof guard.
step-7.acceptance: scripts/phase56-premium-public-auth-smoke.mjs emits required Phase-56 markers and validates route split, CTA, auth states, canonical tariff preview, logo visibility, reduced-motion markers, redaction, and protected deploy/install guard.

step-8.action: Run frontend build, screenshots, smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and protected surface guard.
step-8.acceptance: Frontend build, Phase-56 smoke, Phase-44/46/50/52/53/55 regression smokes, screenshot dimension checks, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected deploy/install guard passed locally.

step-9.acceptance: M-073, V-M-073, M-009, M-071, graph-index, plan-index, verification-index, and current-status record Phase-56 pass-local evidence.

execution-evidence.command-9: Chrome headless production-preview screenshots for /, /login, /register, /forgot-password, /reset-password, /verify-email at 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, 1440x900 plus reduced-motion 390x844.
execution-evidence.command-10: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose guard.
execution-evidence.result: PASS: public landing route, /dashboard protected cabinet route split, visible logo auth routes, canonical tariff preview, email-proof guard copy, screenshot matrix, regression smokes, and protected surface guard passed locally.
execution-evidence.protected-surface: No install.sh, deploy, nginx, docker-compose.yml, deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml, .env.example, backend registration, billing runtime, VPN, MTProto, or runtime topology changes.

Phase-56.acceptance: Public and auth surfaces feel premium and remain safe, compact, and truthful.
